---
title: "**FAQ for users on the DST servers**"
author: "Cæcilie Stilling Denholt & Mikkel Porsborg Andersen"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%d. %B %Y')`"
output:
  pdf_document
editor_options: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

![](faq_pic.jpg)

```{=tex}
\newpage
\tableofcontents
\newpage
```
## Can I contact Statistics Denmark (DST)/The Danish Health Data Authority (SDS) for support?

Please, adhere to not contacting DST or SDS, as we will be billed for
any contact to these.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Moving codes between the project folders

Codes can be moved between project folders if they don’t contain micro
data (see [**Rules of
Engagement**](https://github.com/ctpteam/DST/blob/master/Programming%20guidance/rulesOfEngagement.pdf).

One R-file and one SAS-file can be moved. If more files need to be
moved, please gather them in one file.

Additionally, please adhere to the procedure
described in [**Rules of
Engagement**](https://github.com/ctpteam/DST/blob/master/Programming%20guidance/rulesOfEngagement.pdf).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Export of figures and tables from project folders to external environments

Figures and tables can be exported for publication. In accordance with
your certification, familiarize yourself with the rules for exportation
of data from the servers at:
[**dst.dk**](https://www.dst.dk/da/TilSalg/Forskningsservice/hjemtagelse-af-analyseresultater)

When you and at least one other researcher has looked through the data,
ask a steering committee member (older) to export the data. If this is not possible, ask
your steering committee member to facilitate contact to another researcher with rights
to export (see [**Users and
exporters**](https://github.com/ctpteam/DST/blob/master/Users%20and%20exporters/User%20And%20Exporter.xlsx))
and supply the following information:

-   The path for the place of the file(s) that is to be exported

-   A short description of the file content(s)

-   The names of the researchers, who have checked the files for micro
    data

Do not ask someone to send something home **that is not ready for
publication**, and do adhere to the procedure as described in [**Rules of
Engagement**](https://github.com/ctpteam/DST/blob/master/Programming%20guidance/rulesOfEngagement.pdf).

Be aware that it's not a task for the network administrators to export results.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Upload programs to a project folder

Like for the exports, programs for upload cannot contain microdata.
When you and at least one other researcher has looked through the program,
ask a steering committee member (older) to send the following information
to the network administrators:

-   A short description of the programs content

-   The names of the researchers, who have checked the files for microdata

One R-file and one SAS-file can be moved. If more files need to be
moved, please gather them in one file.

When checking for microdata, please adhere to the procedure described in
[**Rules of
Engagement**](https://github.com/ctpteam/DST/blob/master/Programming%20guidance/rulesOfEngagement.pdf).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Upload data to the project folder

You should never upload data before you have permission from your
supervisor and responsible steering committee member (older).

Please see the guide at Github: **Uploading external data to the DST
servers**

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Resetting a password for DST severs

You can reset your password at remote.dst.dk (the same place you choose
and log in to the servers). Click “Forgot Password” → press “Reset" → a
new password will be sent to your e-mail within a few minutes → click
“Change Password” → fill in the information (NB, along with the password
requirements (**12 characters including both capitalized letters, small
letters, numbers, and symbols**), the password cannot be the same as it
was before resetting it) → press “Submit”

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Changing a password for DST servers

Passwords must be changed every year. The length and complexity
requirements are: **12 characters including both capitalized letters,
small letters, numbers, and symbols**. You can change your password at
remote.dst.dk (the same place you choose and log in to the servers).
Click “Change Password” → fill in the information (NB, along with the
password requirements, the password cannot be the same as it was) →
press “Submit”

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Changing e-mail or phone number for DST-login

To change to the e-mail or phone number connected to your login at
remote.dst.dk: Log in to DDV → press "Profil" in the upper right corner
→ fill in required information

Please note, that the e-mail address has to be from an approved institution (au.dk,
cancer.dk, ku.dk, regionh.dk, regionsjaelland.dk, regsj.dk, rm.dk, rn.dk, rsyd.dk,
sdu.dk, ssi.dk or aau.dk)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Certification on DDV

When required to perform the certification for the DST servers in DDV,
you can find it at remote.dst.dk → "Læring og Certificering". You'll
receive notice from DST when your certification is expiring.

Note: if you are not certified when we do the annual closing of users,
your access will be closed as well.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Annual survey

Every year a survey is sent out to all users in the network. The survey
is designed to close users, that no longer need access to the servers.
If the survey is not filled out within the deadline, the user will be
closed. New users will also receive the survey, when getting access to
the servers, and they also risk being closed again if the survey is not
sent back.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Why do I not have access? And what do I do?

If you either haven't completed the annual survey, haven't completed
your certification on DDV, or your supervisor/steering committee member (older) has asked you to be
closed, you will have your access closed.

If you need to regain access you'll need to contact your supervisor,
which should provide the administrators with the following information:

-   The steering committee member (older) approving the access
-   The project folder(s) you need access to
-   Fill out survey and complete certification (which will be send to
    you)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Creating a new user at DST

Creating a user on a project folder, whether it is a new or an existing
user, requires first and foremost approval from either of the following
steering committee members (olders) (Christian Torp-Pedersen, Lars Køber, Gunnar Gislason,
Henrik Enghusen Poulsen, Emil Fosbøl, Fredrik Folke, Kristian Kragholm,
Morten Schou, Helle Collatz Christensen, Charlotte Andersson,
Mikkel Porsborg Andersen or Peter Rossing).

After being approved, the following information must be submitted to the
administrators **by the steering committee member (older)**:

1.  Number of the project folder, where access is needed

2.  Researcher's full name

3.  Researcher's email address (must be from an approved institution)

4.  Ident (if the user has a previous agreement (brugeraftale) with DST)

5.  Mobile number (must be a Danish phone number)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Access to a specific project folder at Statistics Denmark

If you want access to a project folder you'll need to contact your
steering committee member (older). They should provide the administrators with the following
information:

-   The steering committee member (older) approving the access
-   The project folder(s) you need access to
-   The ident of the user needing access

Note: if the project folder contains more than 100 users, access cannot
be granted.

Please also note, that you can only get access to folders you actually work on.
When getting access to a new project folder, please consider if you still need
access to the old project folder.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## When is the registry data on the project folder updated?

Once a year we get updated registry data to our project database, and we
automatically update the project folders after receiving the data. If
the project folder is not approved by SDS to get continuous date
updates, the data on the project folder will not be automatically
updated and we have to reapply for the data at SDS. If you need updated
data, and it's not found on the project folder, please contact your
responsible steering committee member (older).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Using SundK/RKKP-data

Even though there are several SundK/RKKP registers available on the project
folders, it is required to apply for approval from RKKP to use a
registry **for individual projects**.

Please see the guide at Github: **Applying for RKKP data**

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Available data on the projects folders

You will be able to see which datasets are available on
each individual folder through DDV at remote.dst.dk → 'Mit overblik' →
the project folder number → 'Bestillinger'.
